rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the FLAW in CWE122_Heap_Based_Buffer_Overflow__char_type_overrun_memcpy_01_bad at about line 42 using memcpy with sizeof(*structCharVoid), which copies the size of the whole struct into charFirst and overwrites voidSecond. They explained that this corrupts the pointer and leads to a crash when printed.
rationale.local_fix: They explicitly changed the memcpy size to sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst), limiting the copy to the destination member's size.
rationale.global_fix: They suggested coding standards and static analysis but did not provide a bounded field copy helper or a typed buffer wrapper to prevent confusing struct size with field size.
